Scenario: Login with valid email and password
Preconditions: Registered user exists; Firebase and backend are reachable
Test Steps: Launch app; open Login; enter valid credentials; tap Sign In
Expected Result: User is authenticated, lands on MainTabs/Home, and session tokens are stored

Scenario: Login with invalid password
Preconditions: Registered user exists
Test Steps: Launch app; open Login; enter valid email and wrong password; tap Sign In
Expected Result: User stays on Login screen and an authentication error is shown

Scenario: Register a new account with valid details
Preconditions: Email is not already used
Test Steps: Open Register; fill name, email, password; submit
Expected Result: Account is created, verification is triggered, and user can continue into the app flow

Scenario: Prevent registration with an existing email
Preconditions: Existing account already uses the email
Test Steps: Open Register; submit a name, reused email, and password
Expected Result: Registration is blocked and a clear duplicate-email message is shown

Scenario: Send password reset link for a valid email
Preconditions: Registered user email exists
Test Steps: Open Forgot Password; enter email; submit
Expected Result: Reset email request succeeds and confirmation message is shown

Scenario: Remember Me stores user email for the next login
Preconditions: Remember Me option is visible
Test Steps: Log in with Remember Me enabled; log out; reopen app
Expected Result: Saved email is prefilled or available on the next login attempt

Scenario: Logout clears session and returns to auth flow
Preconditions: User is currently logged in
Test Steps: Open Profile Settings; tap Logout
Expected Result: User is signed out, protected screens are blocked, and app returns to Login/Auth flow

Scenario: Login with Google or Apple succeeds when OAuth is configured
Preconditions: Google or Apple OAuth is configured for the app
Test Steps: Open Login; choose Google or Apple sign-in; complete provider flow
Expected Result: User lands in the app with profile data synced and session stored

Scenario: Home screen loads profile, seasonal content, and recipe data
Preconditions: User is logged in and APIs respond
Test Steps: Sign in; wait for Home screen to finish loading
Expected Result: Home screen shows user context, seasonal section, and recipe/recommendation content

Scenario: Home screen handles API failure without crashing
Preconditions: Backend is unavailable or returns error
Test Steps: Open app to Home while APIs fail
Expected Result: Screen shows loader, fallback, or error state gracefully without app crash

Scenario: Recommendation feed reflects recent user activity
Preconditions: User has recent search, save, or cooking activity
Test Steps: Perform recipe activity; return to Home; inspect recommendations
Expected Result: Recommendation feed updates to reflect relevant recent behavior

Scenario: Create hub shows entry points to creation flows
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Steps: Open Create tab
Expected Result: User can access recipe, adaptation, and cookbook/community creation entry points

Scenario: Search from a food memory prompt returns similar dishes
Preconditions: Memory/AI endpoints are reachable
Test Steps: Open Memory tab; enter a food-memory prompt; submit
Test Data: Prompt such as creamy yellow curry from childhood
Expected Result: Similar dishes list is returned and displayed

Scenario: Prevent empty memory prompt submission
Preconditions: Memory screen is open
Test Steps: Leave prompt empty; tap Search/Continue
Expected Result: Validation is shown and no request is sent

Scenario: Selecting a suggested dish opens recipe customization
Preconditions: Memory search has returned suggestions
Test Steps: Tap one suggested dish
Expected Result: RecipeCustomization screen opens with the selected dish context

Scenario: AI-generated dish path loads a generated recipe
Preconditions: Dish id is AI-generated or non-database format
Test Steps: Navigate with an AI-style dish id; wait for load
Expected Result: Generated recipe content loads successfully instead of a database fetch failure

Scenario: Changing serving size rescales ingredients and cooking time
Preconditions: RecipeCustomization is open with recipe data loaded
Test Steps: Increase or decrease servings
Expected Result: Ingredient quantities and any shown cook-time values update consistently

Scenario: Local adaptation suggestions can replace ingredients
Preconditions: Recipe contains ingredients that can be adapted locally
Test Steps: Open adaptation/substitute controls; apply a suggested substitute
Expected Result: Selected ingredient is replaced and updated recipe context is shown

Scenario: User can tick off cooking steps during a session
Preconditions: Cooking flow has started with instructions available
Test Steps: Open Cooking Steps; mark steps as completed
Expected Result: Checked steps are visibly tracked for the current cooking session

Scenario: Done screen can continue to timer or return to main flow
Preconditions: User has reached the Done screen
Test Steps: Open Done screen; choose timer option; repeat and choose return/home option
Expected Result: Both navigation paths work without losing flow context

Scenario: Timer completion routes user to feedback
Preconditions: Timer is started from cooking flow
Test Steps: Start timer; wait for completion
Expected Result: Timer completes and user is routed to the feedback screen

Scenario: Feedback submission succeeds with rating and comment
Preconditions: Completed cooking session exists
Test Steps: Open Feedback; enter rating and comment; submit
Expected Result: Feedback is saved and user is navigated back to the main app flow

Scenario: Feedback falls back gracefully when Firestore is unavailable
Preconditions: Firebase config is missing or Firestore write fails
Test Steps: Complete cooking flow; submit feedback
Expected Result: App uses fallback submission path or shows clear recovery behavior without crash

Scenario: Search by exact dish name returns matching recipes
Preconditions: Search API is reachable
Test Steps: Open Search; enter an exact dish name; submit
Expected Result: Relevant recipe results are displayed

Scenario: Ingredient-intent search falls back to similar dishes when direct recipe list is empty
Preconditions: Search endpoint can return ingredient intent
Test Steps: Search with ingredient phrase that has no direct recipes
Expected Result: Fallback similar dishes or adaptations are shown instead of an empty broken state

Scenario: Search history stores latest unique queries in recency order
Preconditions: AsyncStorage is available
Test Steps: Search multiple queries including a repeated query
Test Data: Queries such as kottu, lamprais, kottu
Expected Result: History shows unique items only, ordered by most recent update

Scenario: Selecting a search result enriches search history metadata
Preconditions: At least one search result is available
Test Steps: Search; open a result; revisit search history
Expected Result: History entry is updated with recipe id, title, and image when available

Scenario: Removing or clearing history updates the list immediately
Preconditions: Search history contains items
Test Steps: Use remove or clear action from search history UI
Expected Result: Removed items disappear immediately and storage reflects the change

Scenario: Seasonal ingredient list loads on screen entry
Preconditions: Seasonal API is reachable
Test Steps: Open Local Adaptation screen
Test Data: Logged-in or guest user with API access
Expected Result: Seasonal items are displayed without manual refresh

Scenario: Searching a dish or ingredient returns substitutions and suggested recipes
Preconditions: Search/adaptation APIs are reachable
Test Steps: Enter dish or ingredient; submit search
Expected Result: Adaptations list and suggested recipes are shown

Scenario: Ingredient guide opens from selected adaptation
Preconditions: Adaptation result contains guide metadata
Test Steps: Tap an ingredient guide option from Local Adaptation
Expected Result: Guide screen opens and displays ingredient details

Scenario: Selecting a seasonal suggestion opens recipe customization with auto-adapt
Preconditions: SeasonalFood suggestions are visible
Test Steps: Tap a seasonal recipe suggestion
Expected Result: RecipeCustomization opens and applies seasonal adaptation context automatically

Scenario: Submit a new adaptation with image upload
Preconditions: User is logged in and upload endpoint works
Test Steps: Open Add Adaptation; fill fields; attach image; submit
Expected Result: Image uploads successfully and adaptation submission completes

Scenario: Block adaptation submission when required fields are missing
Preconditions: Add Adaptation screen is open
Test Steps: Leave one or more required fields empty; tap Submit
Expected Result: Validation message is shown and submission is not sent

Scenario: Submit a new recipe as draft or pending content
Preconditions: User is logged in and Firebase/upload endpoints are available
Test Steps: Open Add Recipe; complete fields; upload image; submit
Expected Result: Recipe is created successfully with draft or pending status

Scenario: Block recipe creation when title, ingredients, or steps are incomplete
Preconditions: Add Recipe screen is open
Test Steps: Leave mandatory fields incomplete; tap Submit
Expected Result: User sees validation and recipe is not created

Scenario: Valid quantity query navigates to scaled recipe results
Preconditions: Scaling API is reachable
Test Steps: Open Smart Scaling; enter quantity query; continue
Test Data: Query such as I have 500g chicken
Expected Result: App navigates to Scaled Recipe Results with parsed request

Scenario: Invalid or ambiguous scaling query shows error state without crashing
Preconditions: Scaling API returns error or no parse
Test Steps: Submit unclear scaling request
Test Data: Query such as some chicken maybe
Expected Result: User sees a safe error or empty state and the screen remains stable

Scenario: Selecting a scaled recipe opens recipe customization
Preconditions: Scaled results are visible
Test Steps: Tap one scaled recipe result
Expected Result: RecipeCustomization opens with selected recipe data

Scenario: Scaled recipe shows recalculated servings, ingredients, and times
Preconditions: RecipeCustomization opened from scaling flow
Test Steps: Inspect values after scaling
Expected Result: Displayed servings, ingredient quantities, and time-related values match the scaled response

Scenario: Digital cookbook loads user recipes, cookbooks, and saved items
Preconditions: User is logged in and Firebase config is present
Test Steps: Open Library/Digital Cookbook screen
Expected Result: User recipes, cookbooks, and saved collections are listed

Scenario: User can select recipes for a new cookbook and continue
Preconditions: At least one eligible recipe exists
Test Steps: Open cookbook creation flow; select one or more recipes; continue
Expected Result: Selected recipes are retained and next step opens

Scenario: Cover setup validates required cookbook metadata
Preconditions: User is in cookbook creation flow
Test Steps: Leave title or author empty; tap continue; then retry with valid data
Expected Result: Invalid data is blocked; valid data lets user continue

Scenario: Cookbook summary publishes the cookbook successfully
Preconditions: Previous cookbook setup steps are completed
Test Steps: Review summary; submit/publish cookbook
Expected Result: Cookbook is saved successfully and success navigation is shown

Scenario: Published recipe page displays full recipe details
Preconditions: A published recipe item is available
Test Steps: Open a published recipe from library/community
Expected Result: Page shows title, image, ingredients, instructions, and related metadata

Scenario: Draft recipe page displays draft details correctly
Preconditions: A draft recipe item is available
Test Steps: Open a draft recipe from the library
Expected Result: Draft details render correctly without missing-field crashes

Scenario: Community cookbook introduction flow opens reference content
Preconditions: Community module is accessible
Test Steps: Open community cookbook introduction; navigate to reference content
Expected Result: Reference screen opens with expected guidance content

Scenario: Community recipe description form accepts details and moves to next step
Preconditions: Community submission flow is open
Test Steps: Fill recipe description fields; continue
Expected Result: Form accepts valid input and navigates forward

Scenario: Thank-you screen appears after successful community submission
Preconditions: Community submission completed successfully
Test Steps: Complete community submission flow
Expected Result: User sees success/thank-you confirmation screen

Scenario: Profile screen loads current user details
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Steps: Open Profile Settings
Expected Result: User name, email, and profile details are displayed correctly

Scenario: Update display name successfully
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Steps: Open Edit Profile; change display name; save
Expected Result: Updated name is persisted and reflected on profile/home surfaces

Scenario: Upload or replace profile photo successfully
Preconditions: User is logged in and image picker/upload works
Test Steps: Open Edit Profile; choose a new photo; save
Expected Result: Profile photo is uploaded and visible after refresh

Scenario: Change password with correct current password
Preconditions: User is logged in and knows current password
Test Steps: Open Change Password; enter current and new password; submit
Expected Result: Password update succeeds and user remains in valid session

Scenario: Change email and prompt re-verification
Preconditions: User is logged in and knows current password if required
Test Steps: Open Change Email; enter current password and new email; submit
Test Data: Valid current password and new email
Expected Result: Email is updated, verification is requested, and user sees confirmation

Scenario: Expired session redirects user back to login
Preconditions: Session token is invalid or expired
Test Steps: Open a protected screen with an expired session
Expected Result: App clears session safely and returns user to auth flow

Scenario: Notification list loads for the current user
Preconditions: User is logged in and notification data exists
Test Steps: Open Notifications
Expected Result: List of notifications is displayed in correct order

Scenario: Toggle notification preferences and save them
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Steps: Open Notification Settings; change toggles; save; reopen screen
Expected Result: Selected settings persist after reopening the screen

Scenario: Disabled recommendation notifications suppress future recommendation alerts
Preconditions: User has notifications enabled initially
Test Steps: Disable recommendation notifications; trigger recommendation generation; inspect notifications
Expected Result: Recommendation alerts are not shown while the setting remains disabled
